summary: Fixed systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES blockers for M4 objective-complete gating, orderly-withdrawal grain source accounting, WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate read-model copying, and failed-extraction coverage.

tests_run.result: 1 file and 10 tests passed, including R2 regressions for premature objective-complete rejection, nonmatching grain source IDs, read-model aliasing, and failed extraction.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched; command/query hash d275faa4; save/load hash 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 32 root Vitest files and 222 tests passed; web 1 file and 3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-WITHDRAWAL-POSTWAR-HANDOFF-001 and REVIEW-R2 handoff JSON
tests_run.result: Task JSON and REVIEW-R2 handoff JSON parsed successfully with expected status, route, required keys, and tests_run shape.

decisions: Objective-complete withdrawal now requires a completed campaign plus a surrendered siege result reference matching the same target and march before WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate creation.
Orderly withdrawal grain return now uses the campaign grain reservation source ledger and rejects unauditable carried grain instead of treating DistrictId as PopulationGroupId.
sim.list-m4-war-outcomes now deep-copies outcome reasonCodes and nested postwar candidate validM3Methods/reasonCodes before exposing DTOs.
Failed extraction remains deterministic through the existing supply/loss casualty rule and now has focused conservation and reason-code coverage.
risks: R3 remains: authoritative M4 command behavior and DTO read-model boundaries changed and require systems_architect review.
The fix intentionally stays inside sim-core/tests/coordination paths; no protocol or save-format package changes were made for R2.
requested_action: Review the R2 fixes for the prior P1/P2 blockers and either ACCEPT or REQUEST_CHANGES.
